title: Tokenization Could Mirror $20T ETF Boom, Ondo Exec Says
summary: Tokenization is setting the stage for smarter, automated investing, according to John Hoffman, Ondo's new head of portfolio products. This trend could replicate the massive growth seen in ETFs, which now hold over $20 trillion in assets.
body:
Tokenization—the process of turning real-world assets into digital tokens on a blockchain—is poised to revolutionize investing, according to John Hoffman, Ondo's new head of portfolio products. Hoffman believes this technology is laying the groundwork for autonomous investing and real-time portfolio management, much like how exchange-traded funds (ETFs) transformed traditional finance.

Hoffman points to the $20 trillion ETF market as evidence of how asset tokenization could scale. By using blockchain technology, tokenization allows for fractional ownership, increased liquidity, and automated management of assets. The convergence of blockchain and AI could further enhance these capabilities, enabling smarter investment strategies and more efficient portfolio management.

For everyday investors, this means greater access to a wider range of assets, potentially lower fees, and more personalized investment options. Tokenization could democratize investing by allowing smaller investors to participate in markets that were previously out of reach, such as real estate or private equity.

As this trend gains traction, investors should watch for new tokenized asset products and platforms that integrate AI for automated portfolio management. The next few years could see significant growth in this space, making it an area worth keeping an eye on for those looking to diversify their investments.
